title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 16 October 2024.

items.title: **Xbox Partner Preview** digital showcase back this week with 25 minutes of games
bullets: Including Like A Dragon and Alan Wake 2 DLC
6pm in the UK/10am PT on Thursday 17th October
Will feature "over a dozen" new trailers across its 25-minute-ish runtime

items.title: Leaked trailer for Amazon's **Secret Level** reveals Arnold Schwarzenegger and Keanu Reeves
**Final Fantasy 11** responsible for the birth of Discord, its creator says
bullets: Discord creator Stanislav Vishnevskiy discussed the origins of the platform: he was "obsessed" with Final Fantasy 11 and developed a prototype software to use while playing.

items.title: **Steam Next Fest** is now live, featuring ‘thousands of free demos’
bullets: The digital event is running until October 21st

items.title: Mobile game **Destiny: Rising** announced by NetEase and Bungie
bullets: “Experience top-tier sci-fi shooting action in FPP or TPP as you embark on a new adventure in an alternate Destiny timeline. Play as these unique characters to defend future Earth and reclaim humanity’s future!”
Free-to-play
Pre-registration is now open ahead of the launch of its first closed alpha test, which is scheduled to begin on November 1 in the US and Canada.

items.title: **Tron: Catalyst** is a new isometric action game from Bithell Games
bullets: Disney Tron: Catalyst is a story-driven isometric action game coming to PC, PS5, Xbox Series S/X and Nintendo Switch via Devolver Digital’s Big Fan Games publishing label.
"Coming 2025"

items.title: **Games Done Quick** raises over $45,000 for hurricane relief
PlayStation horror **Until Dawn'**s live-action movie hits cinemas next April
bullets: 25th April 2025

items.title: **Warhammer 40K: Space Marine 2** shows off formidable new Hierophant Bio-Titan Tyranid in Season 2 trailer
bullets: Coming "very, very soon"

items.title: **Hamlet** film made in **GTA Online** coming to US & UK cinemas and global streaming services
bullets: Courtesy of UK distributor Tull Stories, the film (Grand Theft Hamlet) will be in cinemas across the UK and Ireland from 6th December 2024.

That follows its UK premiere at the London Film Festival later this month, and other film festival appearances at Bath Film Festival (21st October), Cambridge Film Festival (24th & 25th October), and Leeds Film Festival (8th & 9th November).

items.title: **Against the Storm** has sold 1.2m copies
bullets: In addition to the milestone, Publisher Hooded Horse also announced that Workers & Resources: Soviet Republic sold over 600,000 units, Norland over 300,000 copies since its EA launch in July, and more

items.title: **Rare**'s Craig Duncan to become head of Xbox Game Studios as Alan Hartman retires
bullets: Hartman leaves after a 36-year career that included forming Turn 10 and launching the Forza series

Duncan has led Rare for the best part of 14 years, overseeing the studio's Kinect titles and the launch of its live service hit Sea of Thieves, which has had more than 40 million players since it launched in 2018.

items.title: **Riot Games** eliminates 32 roles
bullets: Riot Games has confirmed 27 layoffs from its League team, and five further redundancies from its "publishing side."

items.title: **Dragon Ball: Sparking Zero** sells 3m copies in 24 hours
**Bandai Namco** is reportedly cutting workforce following cancellation of "several" titles
bullets: It's alleged 200 of its 1300 employees have been moved into these "expulsion rooms" – or "oidashi beya" – and almost half of these have since voluntarily resigned.
Bandai Namco has reportedly also cancelled several titles, including games featuring the One Piece and Naruto licenses, as well as a contract project with Nintendo
